description: Hand-curated intent classification evaluation set. Matches the row totals reported in Table 5.1 of the capstone report (10 utterances per class). Utterances are paraphrased natural learner phrasings, deliberately including the lexically ambiguous cases (depth/clarification overlap) called out in Section 7.1.4 so the measured behaviour matches the qualitative claim in the report.

schema.label: ground-truth intent class (clarification | objection | depth_request | topic_pivot | example_request)

samples.label: clarification
samples.utterance: Can you explain that again, I don't understand.

samples.label: clarification
samples.utterance: What do you mean by that exactly?

samples.label: clarification
samples.utterance: I'm a bit confused, could you clarify?

samples.label: clarification
samples.utterance: I don't understand what you just said.

samples.label: clarification
samples.utterance: Wait, what does that mean?

samples.label: clarification
samples.utterance: Could you explain this in simpler terms?

samples.label: clarification
samples.utterance: That part is unclear to me.

samples.label: clarification
samples.utterance: I'm lost, can you go over it once more?

samples.label: clarification
samples.utterance: Sorry, I'm confused about the last sentence.

samples.label: clarification
samples.utterance: Hold on, I didn't quite follow that.

samples.label: objection
samples.utterance: But that doesn't seem right at all.

samples.label: objection
samples.utterance: Actually, I think that is wrong.

samples.label: objection
samples.utterance: Hmm, that can't be true.

samples.label: objection
samples.utterance: I disagree with what you just said.

samples.label: objection
samples.utterance: That's incorrect, isn't it?

samples.label: objection
samples.utterance: But surely the opposite is the case.

samples.label: objection
samples.utterance: Wait, that contradicts what you said earlier.

samples.label: objection
samples.utterance: I don't think that's right.

samples.label: objection
samples.utterance: Actually, the textbook says the opposite.

samples.label: objection
samples.utterance: That isn't quite right though.

samples.label: depth_request
samples.utterance: Could you go deeper into how that works?

samples.label: depth_request
samples.utterance: Tell me more about why this happens.

samples.label: depth_request
samples.utterance: I'd like a more detailed explanation.

samples.label: depth_request
samples.utterance: Can you elaborate on the mechanism?

samples.label: depth_request
samples.utterance: Why is that the case under the hood?

samples.label: depth_request
samples.utterance: How come this works that way?

samples.label: depth_request
samples.utterance: Go further into the details please.

samples.label: depth_request
samples.utterance: More on that last point would help.

samples.label: depth_request
samples.utterance: Expand on the second step a bit.

samples.label: depth_request
samples.utterance: I want to understand this in more depth.

samples.label: topic_pivot
samples.utterance: Let's move on to a different topic now.

samples.label: topic_pivot
samples.utterance: Can we change the subject please?

samples.label: topic_pivot
samples.utterance: I want to skip ahead to the next section.

samples.label: topic_pivot
samples.utterance: What about the chapter on traversals instead?

samples.label: topic_pivot
samples.utterance: Let's talk about something else.

samples.label: topic_pivot
samples.utterance: Move on to the next topic.

samples.label: topic_pivot
samples.utterance: Skip this part, let's go forward.

samples.label: topic_pivot
samples.utterance: Enough of this, what's next?

samples.label: topic_pivot
samples.utterance: New topic - tell me about deletion.

samples.label: topic_pivot
samples.utterance: Change topic to something more advanced.

samples.label: example_request
samples.utterance: Can you give me a concrete example?

samples.label: example_request
samples.utterance: Show me an example of that please.

samples.label: example_request
samples.utterance: For instance, what would that look like?

samples.label: example_request
samples.utterance: Like what? I need a real example.

samples.label: example_request
samples.utterance: Could you give me an example of how this is used?

samples.label: example_request
samples.utterance: What would that look like in practice?

samples.label: example_request
samples.utterance: An example would really help here.

samples.label: example_request
samples.utterance: Could you show me a real-world example?

samples.label: example_request
samples.utterance: Give me a sample to work through.

samples.label: example_request
samples.utterance: Can I see a worked example of this?
